items.title: What EWHF Is
body: EWHF stands for Eat Well. Have Fun.

This is a collection of recipes built around a simple idea: food can support better health without becoming joyless, bland, or precious. The recipes are written for people who still want real meals, real flavor, and the satisfaction of cooking something worth eating.

The focus is low-cholesterol cooking, but the method is not deprivation. EWHF leans on vegetables, beans, grains, herbs, spices, stocks, sauces, browning, roasting, simmering, crisping, and careful seasoning to build flavor. Instead of replacing rich food with sad food, the recipes try to rebuild the pleasure of the dish from the ground up.

Some recipes are very simple. Others take more time because they use technique instead of shortcuts. That might mean cooking dried beans, building a sauce, browning mushrooms properly, reducing stock, crisping a topping, or adding texture in layers. Those steps matter. They are how the food earns its place at the table.

EWHF is not a medical program, and it is not an official nutrition system. It is a practical cooking project with a clear point of view: lower the cholesterol where possible, support better eating patterns, and still make food that feels generous, satisfying, and fun.
